raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> I made a hotel reservation at a web site with a domain name " XXXX XXXX ''. When I received a confirmation email from " Hotel reservations '' I was suspicious and cancelled the reservation. I also notified American Express of the potential scam and was told by an agent that the charge was pending and would not be paid without notification from me. Nevertheless on my XXXX statement there was a charge from " XXXX XXXX '' dated XX/XX/XXXX. When I contacted American Express by phone the agent said it would be investigated. Since then American Express has provided me with copies of documents I have never before seen and claims of action I have never taken from " XXXX '' whom I have never heard of. They now claim that since whomever they paid claimed that I approved payment, I am responsible for a charge of {$880.00} that American Express was told before payment was fradulent. 
By accepting phony " proof '' from an obviously fraudulent entity without careful investigation American Express is aiding and abetting fraud in spite of their claims that cardholders are protected from fraud. I have deducted the {$880.00} from my payments in subsequent months as American Express " continues to investigate. ''
